mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales and housing starts diverge, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side slack, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of months following an industrial production decline, or the signal loses predictive power during stable growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months following inflation acceleration, or rolling IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following payroll growth, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales signals a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate profit expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns when GDP and retail sales move in opposite directions, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >50% of co-decline quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization and strong order backlogs, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following the industrial production threshold crossing, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power during high-volatility regimes.
